_comment: Audit trail of quality decisions and notable build events. Append only.

decisions.decision: Completed the open-source video tooling evaluation: 15 candidates scored against the two-lane videoedit architecture, 7 hands-on spikes (6 passed, 1 blocked by container network policy), 4 research agents plus 1 adversarial verification agent (3 of 8 load-bearing claims upheld, 5 refined).
decisions.rationale: G9 (transcript-to-chapters/cuts) and the flagged-off shorts_reframe and render/export slots need a vetted shortlist before any integration work. Empirical spikes ran against synthetic media sharing ground truth with the committed workshop-footage.srt fixture, so tool accuracy is measured against known-authored cuts and silences.
decisions.explicit_non_action: No integration, no flag changes, no requirements changes; G9 and G10 remain open and the scenario suite's probes still observe them.

decisions.decision: G9 silence/cut detection slot: shortlist ffmpeg silencedetect (top pick), PyAV windowed RMS (no-binary runner-up), degrading to the stdlib SRT gap analysis proven in spike S-0.
decisions.rationale: S-1 measured silencedetect within 0.021s of authored silences; S-4 reproduced it in-process within one 100ms window without any binary; S-0 recovered all three authored gaps from the transcript alone in 21ms.
decisions.explicit_non_action: No integration, no flags, G9 remains open.

decisions.decision: G9 scene/chapter detection slot: shortlist PySceneDetect ContentDetector (top pick), ffmpeg scdet (runner-up with a documented luma-only default), degrading to transcript-derived chapter heuristics.
decisions.rationale: S-3 found all 4 authored cuts frame-exact with zero false positives, including an isoluminant cut that ffmpeg missed; the adversarial pass source-verified the ffmpeg limitation (YUV path scores luma only) and its format=rgb24 workaround.
decisions.explicit_non_action: No integration, no flags, G9 remains open.

decisions.decision: shorts_reframe slot: shortlist MoviePy v2 for the mechanical crop/trim/encode half (ffmpeg crop filter as runner-up), degrading to emitting crop parameters into the edit package without rendering.
decisions.rationale: S-5 produced an ffprobe-verified 9:16 crop with a fully self-contained pip install (imageio-ffmpeg bundles its own ffmpeg). Maintenance is the watch item: no release since 2025-05, no commits since 2025-09 (adversarially verified).
decisions.explicit_non_action: No integration; the shorts_reframe flag stays off.

decisions.decision: render/export slot: shortlist MLT XML emission as a second Lane A format (Shotcut native, Kdenlive substrate) with optional melt rendering; ffmpeg direct encode as runner-up; auto-editor conditionally shortlisted pending FCPXML round-trip validation. Kdenlive and Shotcut are interchange targets, not dependencies: both were verified to expose no edit-automation API (project file + melt is the surface), which confirms the two-lane thesis.
decisions.rationale: Group B/D research verified MLT XML as the shared substrate across Shotcut and Kdenlive and melt as the developer-documented headless export path for both. auto-editor emits exactly our Lane A formats but its S-2 spike was network-blocked, so the fcpxml.py round-trip is the top open validation item. LosslessCut failed the headless gate (GUI-bound automation) and is documented as a manual companion only.
decisions.explicit_non_action: No integration; the render/export flag stays off; no MLT writer is built by this decision.

decisions.decision: Adopted five governance patterns from the maintainer's prior meeting-evidence system (reviewed offline; source document not committed): (1) a five-mode evidence acquisition ladder (direct_connector / export_bundle / excerpt_only / internal_context_only / hybrid_reconciliation) added to the connector model and registry; (2) a field-level memory safety model (raw / conditional / derived_reviewed / safe write classes) added to the pipeline engine; (3) identity confidence separated from claim confidence in the verification envelope; (4) artifact_completeness (minimal/partial/rich) plus per-item provenance required on evidence bundles; (5) explicit stop conditions as first-class workflow outcomes, implemented in deal-review.js.
decisions.rationale: The patterns operationalize the existing no-fabrication and human-review protocols at finer granularity: silent downgrades, identity laundering via strong claims, and confident-wrong CRM writes were previously discouraged by prose but not structurally named. All changes are additive; no schema field was removed and drift guard invariants 14 to 17 are unaffected.

decisions.decision: Closed scenario-suite gaps G9 and G10. Promoted the stdlib SRT pause analysis (proven in the P26 S-0 spike and previously runner-owned evidence code) into product capability: shared/docintel/transcripts.py gained gap_metrics() (inter-segment silence detection) and suggest_chapters() (chapter boundary proposal from silences plus words_per_minute drops; titles always null, named by the model or human from transcript text). Added the footage_breakdown routing classification to creator-core (Content lane, video-development spoke) and a footage-analysis atom as the realizer. tools/scenario_check.py op_gap_metrics now delegates to the product function; scenario S5 flipped from ambiguous routing with G9/G10 evidence legs to a present-routing product assertion.
decisions.rationale: P26 shortlisted media tools (PySceneDetect, ffmpeg, auto-editor, PyAV) for a future integration phase but proved the zero-dependency stdlib floor recovers the authored silence structure exactly. Shipping the floor first gives the raw-footage breakdown request an honest, offline, zero-token capability today and preserves the degradation chain the P26 report specified (tool -> ffmpeg-only -> stdlib floor). Media tool integration remains a future phase; no new dependency, flag, or connector was added.

decisions.decision: Integrated the P26 shortlist as optional, runtime-detected backends over the P28 stdlib floor. Silence/cut slot: tools/videoedit/mediaprobe.py detect_silence (ffmpeg silencedetect -> PyAV windowed RMS -> transcripts.gap_metrics). Scene/chapter slot: detect_scenes (PySceneDetect ContentDetector -> ffmpeg scdet with the luma caveat attached -> transcripts.suggest_chapters; titles always null). shorts_reframe slot: tools/videoedit/reframe.py (crop geometry pure math always available; render gated on shorts_reframe walking MoviePy -> ffmpeg crop -> refusal-with-valid-params). render/export slot: tools/videoedit/mltxml.py (MLT XML build/parse/validate as the second Lane A format; render gated on the new media_render flag, added to APP_DRIVING so it also requires video_editing_enabled, walking melt -> ffmpeg cut-list -> honest handoff). Every result carries computed_by, backend_chain, and parameters. Three new atoms (silence-scan, scene-scan, shorts-reframe), two new flags (mlt_timeline_export, media_render), four MCP tools, preflight detection for all backends, requirements-videoedit.txt (optional only). otio_core.merge fixed to union incoming gaps[] and adopt an enabled reframe directive (both were silently dropped). Live-verified against P26 goldens: ffmpeg silencedetect within 0.003s, PyAV exact, PySceneDetect all 4 cuts including the isoluminant one, scdet 3 of 4 with caveat, MoviePy and ffmpeg renders ffprobe-verified 404x720.
decisions.rationale: Ships the P26 per-slot recommendations with the exact degradation chains the evaluation specified, keeping the repo's optional-dependency posture: nothing new is required, every path degrades to the P28 transcript floor or an honest refusal, and provenance makes fabricated timings structurally impossible. Explicitly NOT integrated or unverified: auto-editor (binary bootstrap still 403-blocked through the proxy; conditionally shortlisted only), melt render execution (no melt in the build container; gate-tested only), macOS paths, editor-side .mlt opening, and otio-kdenlive-adapter reading our Shotcut-flavored MLT (adapter expects Kdenlive docproperties; recorded as failed bonus check). Evidence: docs/video-tooling-integration-evidence.json.

decisions.decision: Built the accounting bucket. Record store: pipeline/finance/ standalone invoice records (many per deal, line items, terms_snapshot frozen at issue), cost estimates, and cost actuals, resolving the invoice drift toward the engine's standalone model (deal.invoice is now a denormalized summary plus invoice_refs[]). Structured money terms added additively to deal (v0.3.0) and contract schemas: payment_terms_structured (net days, anchor, deposit, late penalty type/rate/grace, kill fee), revenue_share, commission, ip_license_fee, all filled only from quoted evidence; playbook gained pricing_and_rates and revenue_share_and_commission families. tools/finance.py is the second offline compute lane instance (imports obligations.py date machinery; Decimal ROUND_HALF_UP; 44-check selftest): AR aging with left-inclusive buckets and rolled chase dates (contractual due dates never rolled), late-penalty accrual (flat / percent-per-month, full elapsed months, unsupported structures refused), revenue-share/commission clamps from reported basis figures only, cost rollups with expense/capex split, proposal price floors (max of cost floor and negotiation floor), deterministic invoice ids, sha256 manifest, CREATOR_OS_ROOT sandbox; writes gated on finance_management + invoice_generation. New finance-desk spoke with invoice-generate, ar-review, cost-estimate, proposal-price atoms; four hub classifications; document-studio invoice artifact type; invoice-status upgraded to standalone records and six states. G8 closed deliberately (structured benchmark rows, metric rows sourced-or-null; suite at 7 gaps). Personal rate card template with deal-debrief proposal-only feedback loop. Fifth agent role cost-researcher (envelope schema, web-intel L2/L3, observed-or-null prices) with the cost library and homedepot/lowes vendor sources registered via source_currency.py. Five MCP tools (32 total). Verbatim financial boundary (NOT TAX, ACCOUNTING, OR INVESTMENT ADVICE) in finance-engine.md and protocols/safety.md, with a consequential-action gate before any external money commitment.
decisions.rationale: The creator needed AR tracking, invoicing matched to deals/contracts/terms (net days, late penalties), projected costs, standardized pricing feeding proposals and contract drafts, and structured revenue-share/commission/IP-licensing money terms. Everything reuses the established disciplines: the obligations date machinery rather than duplicated math, records-or-null over estimation for every figure, read-scans always available with writes flag-gated, committed blank templates with gitignored .local data (invariant 19), human confirmation before anything money-facing leaves the system, and findings-as-contract for the G8 close. Explicitly out of scope: sending invoices or payments, bank reconciliation, multi-currency math, any tax computation, dashboard finance UI, auto-dunning.

decisions.decision: Ship the four P30 follow-up finance features (cash-flow projection, dashboard AR tab, payment reconciliation, dunning drafts) on a machine-enforced privacy boundary built first.
decisions.rationale: The prior boundary was filename convention only: no gitignore rules for exports, no content scanning, no commit hygiene enforcement. P31 layers accident prevention (allowlist-invert gitignore), force-add detection (invariant 20), content scanning (secret_scan.py, invariant 21, CI), per-clone git hooks, a CI commit-message backstop bounded by a policy SHA, and a written non-negotiable policy. Features then ship with structural safeties: redaction for anything leaving the machine, proposal-only reconciliation with an in-repo non-local CSV refusal, and dunning drafts that are never sent.
decisions.anti_over_share_policy: Commit messages, PR bodies, and issue comments carry no session links, personal emails, real amounts, real counterparty names, credentials, or PII. Author email is the GitHub noreply address. Enforced by the commit-msg hook and the CI backstop; exemptions only via tools/secret-scan-allowlist.json with a written reason. No history rewrite: the policy applies forward of the boundary SHA recorded in the allowlist file.
evidence: negative tests: gitignore block, force-add trip, hook rejections, CSV refusal
decisions.explicit_non_action: No history rewrite; no new dependencies; no entropy scanning; no auto-sent dunning; no bank API integrations.

decisions.decision: Close all seven remaining scenario-suite capability gaps (G1 to G7): the CRM read lane, the seasonal canonical source, and the media-kit market critique path. The P24 gap ledger is now empty (G1 to G10 all closed).
decisions.rationale: The findings-as-contract suite pinned these as the documented backlog. Each close lands atomically with its probe flip and a strengthened product leg so the suite never drifts silently.
what_shipped: tools/accounts.py: offline, read-only CRM resolver (tiered exact/alias/substring/difflib/category matching, never auto-picks past alias), contacts reader, and verbatim deal-status reader; CREATOR_OS_ROOT sandbox; redaction for PII; 27-check selftest
account-resolve, contact-lookup, deal-status atoms; account-manager gains a contact_lookup action and account-resolve step; deal-pipeline gains a deal-status read step
account-schema.json v0.2.0 reconciled to the pipeline engine (brand_category enum, secondary_contacts, relationship_health, aliases, channel_preferences, deal_history_summary, renewal_candidate)
hub classifications account_read, deal_status, content_critique with routing rows

canonical-sources/seasonal-aesthetic.md and the seasonal.json seasonal-windows entry with resolved ISO dates (reference_year, annual recurrence); engine/atom/JSON reconciled; drift invariant 22 validates frontmatter load refs and canonical-sources joined KNOWN_ROOTS
mediakit-critique atom (benchmark-compare per metric plus structural review; structural_only degraded mode when benchmarks are unsourced) routed via content_critique to partnership-mediakit
MCP tools contact_lookup and deal_status (read-only, PII-masked); runner OPS accounts.resolve/contacts/deal_status
decisions.privacy_posture: Contacts are PII: account/deal reads are read-only, output is for the human operator on-machine, and MCP tools plus the CLI redact names and emails for anything quoted off-machine. No new tracked files under pipeline/; fixtures are fictional with .example emails.
decisions.explicit_non_action: No CRM write paths; no nickname dictionary or phonetic matching; no combined crm_scan MCP tool; no sourcing of the null benchmark metric rows; quality-review scope unchanged.

decisions.decision: Audit and modernize the source-currency system: fix its config bugs, close the dependency blind spot, and add a token-free periodic-update lane.
decisions.rationale: A two-agent audit found the registry seeded but dormant (all entries never checked), real cadence bugs (legal/cost categories missing from traversal-config so treated as 7-day-due; stale MCP-spec and TikTok URLs; duplicate and placeholder entries), and a structural blind spot: pip packages, system binaries, and MCP servers added across P8-P32 were untracked and drifted silently. The user required accurate checks and token-free mundane updating.
what_shipped: tools/registry_io.py: single shared registry writer imported by source_currency.py, traversal_engine.py, dependency_currency.py
source_currency.py update-source (corrections) and check --detect-changes[/--apply] (conditional GET + sha256 content-change detection; unchanged/first_seen stamped token-free, changed queued)

tools/dependency_currency.py: token-free version-drift checker querying PyPI JSON + GitHub Releases, deterministic drift (major bump = breaking), advisory offline degradation, --apply stamping; selftest

registry schema extension: software-dependency and mcp-server categories + fields upstream_api/check_url/package/pinned_constraint/validated_version/latest_seen; dependency-sources-seed.json (12 pip + 6 binaries + 10 MCP servers)
traversal-config overrides for legal-authority/cost-vendor and the two dep categories; allowlist +tiktok.com/copyright.gov/law.cornell.edu/github.com/ecfr.gov; traverse-all skips non-web categories
content sources: FTC eCFR 16 CFR 255, three cost vendors, a github-seo methodology upstream; data-currency-map.json classifies every canonical file (watched/static/dated/tool-managed)
drift invariant 23: every requirements package and every MCP-backed connector must have a registry entry; CI currency-report job (read-only, weekly); docs/CURRENCY.md runbook
dependency baseline --apply stamped the reachable pip deps, ending their dormancy
decisions.accuracy_mechanism: pip -> PyPI JSON API (exact version + date); GitHub tool/MCP -> GitHub Releases API; binary/manual -> advisory with the pinned/validated baseline and check URL. Web content change -> conditional GET + sha256 vs the hash stored on the entry. All deterministic Python, no model tokens for the unchanged/undrifted majority.
decisions.explicit_non_action: No auto-upgrading of pins (drift reported, human bumps); CI is report-only (no registry auto-write); no scraping where a structured API exists; intentionally-static creator-local files stay static; the web-content baseline --apply runs on first deployment (proxy-dependent reachability).

decisions.decision: Add an offline, cited residential construction and DIY knowledge base scoped to Florida and North Carolina: a per-trade dictionary, first-principles calculators, code and edition lookup, diagrams, a public-domain asset crawler, and regularly-updated sources.
decisions.rationale: The creator's DIY home-decor work needs authoritative build reference that a stock model answers from memory and often gets wrong on dimensions, code sections, and jurisdiction. The defining constraint, confirmed by six URL-verified research agents, is that the building codes (IRC/NEC/AWC/APA/ACCA) are copyrighted and non-redistributable (APA even bans AI training). So the base is our own authored, cited prose keyed to code section numbers with links to the free viewers, built from genuinely reusable public-domain and open sources (DOE Building America, NREL BSD-3, Wikidata CC0, Wikimedia), never bulk-copied code text.
decisions.what_shipped: shared/construction-engine.md; the construction/ dictionary (12 trades + glossary, assemblies, fl-nc-specifics, edition-status, diagram-index) indexed by the scoop cache; tools/build_calc.py (8 first-principles calculators, selftest 24/24); construction-lookup, code-lookup, build-calc atoms and the construction-desk spoke, hub-routed as construction_question/code_lookup/build_calc with project-builder consuming them; construction_lookup/code_lookup/build_calc MCP tools; five original CC0 SVG diagrams; tools/construction_fetch.py (public-domain-only crawler that structurally refuses copyrighted hosts, selftest 12/12); 37 sources registered under new building-code (cite-only) and construction-authority currency categories; docs/CONSTRUCTION.md.
decisions.accuracy_mechanism: Every dictionary record carries source_ids, edition-aware code_refs (section + edition + free-viewer url), and the verify-locally boundary; drift invariant 22 enforces those plus per-diagram licenses and diagram_ref resolution. Calculators compute from geometry/physics or public-domain restatements, never copyrighted tables, and cite their section. The crawler cannot bundle copyrighted material (structural host refusal). Edition awareness (FL 2023 8th Ed, NC 2018/2015-IRC with the pending 2024 transition) is tracked via edition-status.json and the data-currency-map watch. No-fabrication: missing values are null and flagged.
decisions.explicit_non_action: No commercial (IBC) content; no redistribution of copyrighted code text or AWC/APA/ICC figures; no scraping of ICC/UpCodes/NFPA; no per-address wind or flood values (link FEMA MSC and the ASCE Hazard Tool); no jurisdictions beyond FL and NC in this phase; no code-compliance or engineering advice.

decisions.decision: Add an offline, source-cited, human-gated project task and obligation tracker for brand deals: event-triggered tasks per deal and contract, backwards-planning from deadlines, a seven-state lifecycle with a first-class waiting-on state, recurrence, two-party approval ping-pong, live/manual shipment tracking as the delivered_at planning anchor, multi-transcript coverage verification of required talking points, payment-milestone billable-readiness into the finance lane, and a pluggable store adapter (local_fs / google_drive / remote_mcp) for cross-surface and cross-AI continuity.
decisions.rationale: Brand deals run on event-triggered, multi-party obligations the creator tracks in their head. The defining constraint the creator set is anti-phantom: there must never be a task the tool identifies that it cannot cite to a specific contract clause, email, user statement, or a named rule grounded in a human artifact. The design reuses the strongest existing assets (obligations date math, finance invoicing/cashflow, docintel transcripts+WER, the verification envelope, connectors/ingest-route, the append-only registry conventions) rather than rebuilding them, and the append-only event log makes a shared Google Drive store concurrency-safe by union-merge rather than last-writer-wins, which is what unlocks the same tasks on claude.ai web, the Claude desktop app, and mobile for a non-technical Mac user with no hosting.
decisions.what_shipped: shared/tasks-engine.md; tools/tasks.py (records, 7-state transition choke point, anti-phantom validate, forward/backward/reverse-plan/feasibility scheduling, RRULE recurrence materialize-on-demand, waiting-on handoff nudge/escalate, ping-pong, billable-readiness, .ics export + reminders digest, store adapter with event-log union-merge + sha256 manifest; selftest 46/46); tools/shipments.py (canonical status enum, EasyPost/Ship24 flag-gated poll over stdlib urllib with env-only keys, manual entry, delivered_at anchor; selftest 15/15); tools/coverage_verify.py (ROVER-style reconciliation with credible-dissent conflicts, FEVER-style tiered coverage with extractive citations and abstention, minority_report; selftest 18/18); four register templates (task-register, recurrence-rules, shipments, payment-schedule) with deliverable_id/approval_state on the deal schema; eight atoms and the task-desk spoke, hub-routed as task_status/task_plan/coverage_check/shipment_update/milestone_bill; MCP tools and a dashboard tasks view; Google Drive/Sheets store backend and an optional remote-MCP transport with cross-AI packaging re-projections; four config capabilities (task_tracking, shipment_tracking, coverage_verification, task_store_backend); drift invariant 24 and the S6 two-party scenario with fixtures; docs/TASK-TRACKER.md and the DEPLOYMENT capability-matrix rows.
decisions.accuracy_mechanism: Every task carries a source of kind document/event_derived/user_stated; tools/tasks.validate_task rejects a missing or malformed source and drift invariant 24 backstops the data at rest. history[] is the append-only source of truth and status/responsible_party/billable_ready are folds over it. Coverage verifies each required point against extractive quotes proven present in the reconciled canonical text and abstains (routing to the human gate) rather than inferring when the semantic tiers are absent; every credible transcript conflict is retained in a minority_report. Shipment delivered_at comes only from the delivered checkpoint. No-fabrication: missing dates/coverage are null and flagged, never inferred.
decisions.explicit_non_action: No auto-send of any email, invoice, nudge, or post (human-gated throughout). No webhook endpoints (carrier polling only). No hosted remote MCP server (the transport option and deploy runbook ship; standing one up is user/host work). Carrier API keys are read from the environment only, never committed or logged. Semantic/NLI coverage tiers are optional and flag-gated; the stdlib core abstains rather than guesses. No legal advice and no judgment of disclosure sufficiency. No blockchain chain of custody; a sha256 content hash is the tamper-evidence.

decisions.decision: Turn the dormant source-currency system into an always-fresh, per-user, self-contained freshness system that keeps every deployment's reference data accurate on every modality (Claude Desktop/MCP, claude.ai web/mobile, Custom GPT, Gemini) without the freshness runtime ever touching GitHub. Add a read-only-baseline + user-controlled overlay model, monitors for connector APIs / AI-surface packaging formats / creator content data / compliance sources, embedded-fact tracking, visible per-surface freshness stamps, a wizard store step, MCP tools, and a local scheduler.
decisions.rationale: Research found the existing currency machinery was built but dormant (126 of 139 sources never stamped, no content-hash baseline ever run, CI read-only with no loop closure, no MCP mutating surface) and that facts duplicated in engine/config prose were untracked, and that the four knowledge-only surfaces had no regeneration path and no visible as-of date. The owner set two hard constraints: the freshness system must never push/pull/propose to GitHub or generate homework for anyone, and each user's refreshed data must live only in a store that user controls. The append-only overlay + union-merge (reused from the P35 task store) makes a shared store concurrency-safe and keeps the repo a read-only, download-only baseline the owner updates only by their own manual choice.
decisions.what_shipped: tools/freshness_overlay.py (read-only-baseline + user overlay: append-only event log, deterministic union-merge, provenance envelope, selector-scoped hashing, RFC 9111 max-age, two-tier SLA, Wayback link-rot, local dashboard, store adapter over local_fs/google_drive/remote_mcp, refreshed-value envelopes, in-platform refresh-agent contract; selftest 30/30); source_currency.py extended with an --overlay path so report/check/detect-changes/dashboard write only to the user's store; data-currency-map.json embedded_fact_files (12 prose/config artifacts + 2 missing seeds) with drift invariant 25; four seed files (33 monitorable sources: api-changelog, a new ai-surface-spec category, content-data, compliance) with the best token-free signal each; tools/build_freshness_bundle.py stamping a visible freshness date on the 11 knowledge digests with drift invariant 26 (selftest 9/9); a wizard /freshness-setup store step; MCP tools currency_scan/currency_detect_changes/freshness_refresh (overlay-only writes); a local launchd/cron scheduler example; the weekly CI currency-report job retired for zero GitHub coupling; docs/FRESHNESS.md.
decisions.accuracy_mechanism: The runtime writes only the user's overlay, never the repo registry and never GitHub; union-merge folds baseline + overlay deterministically. Change detection is conditional-GET + sha256 (selector-scoped where set); refreshed values carry {as_of, source_citation, publish_date} and stale ones are flagged. Drift invariant 25 keeps every embedded fact tied to a real registry source; invariant 26 keeps the downloadable knowledge baseline from silently lagging canonical (its as_of stamp must match the current canonical digest). Licensing: government primary sources are cached freely; copyrighted model codes and gated reports cache the fact/date + source URL only, never the text. No-fabrication: null-and-flag over guessing.
decisions.explicit_non_action: The freshness system performs no GitHub writes of any kind (no commit/push/pull/PR/proposal/update-nag) and no upstream aggregation of any user's findings; the repo is download-only and the owner publishes solely by their own manual choice. No hosted server (remote MCP is opt-in, user-provided). State AI-content law has no unified feed (per-jurisdiction polling, flagged). API keys are env-only, never persisted. Copyrighted code/report text is never cached.

decisions.decision: Add an optional, advisory jurisdictional-overlay bucket on top of the construction knowledge base: given a project location, resolve whether it falls inside a flood zone / historic district / hurricane zone / steep-slope / watershed overlay (geometry, attribute, or versioned-fact) and, when two rules collide, which governs -- escalating genuine legal conflicts to human review. Offline stdlib engine in EPSG:4326, a live FEMA connector behind a second default-off flag, FL + NC data, plugged into the scoop cache and the P36 freshness system. Advisory planning information only, never a legal or permitting determination.
decisions.rationale: The construction base knew what the code says but nothing about WHERE a project is; the user pasted a six-level jurisdictional-overlay architecture (GIS ingestion, CRS, point-in-polygon, overrides, conflict resolution) and asked for a plan to fold the applicable parts into an optional scoop-style bucket. Six cited research agents established that a correct multi-format point-in-polygon is fully doable offline in stdlib + EPSG:4326 (no shapely/geopandas/pyproj), that not every overlay is a polygon (HVHZ and SB 4D are statutory attribute rules; SLR is a versioned fact), that the authoritative conflict model is floor/ceiling preemption + Dillon/Home-Rule authority + lex specialis (LegalRuleML/ACCORD/OpenFisca), and that government GIS is largely public-domain and ArcGIS-REST/OGC queryable with editingInfo/ETag freshness signals. The whole thing reuses the existing cache + currency + construction record/drift patterns and stays advisory + cite-only, matching the repo's discipline.
decisions.what_shipped: tools/geo_overlay.py (stdlib EPSG:4326 point-in-polygon with holes/multipolygon/half-open vertex rule, bbox pre-filter + true ring test, GeoJSON/KML ingest, three overlay kinds, conflict-resolution cascade + W3C PROV audit + human-review escape; selftest 26/26); tools/geo_fetch.py (live FEMA NFHL point flood-zone query + freshness read, gated by jurisdictional_overlay_live default off, no surprise network call; selftest 10/10 offline); the optional canonical-sources/jurisdiction/ bucket (example schema + fl-overlays.json + nc-overlays.json), auto-indexed by the scoop cache; jurisdictional_overlay and jurisdictional_overlay_live config flags (both default off); drift invariant 27 (check_jurisdiction); 14 seeded FL+NC GIS/legal sources under a new jurisdiction-gis cadence category with currency-map + P36 freshness wiring; MCP tools jurisdiction_resolve and overlay_conflict; docs/JURISDICTION-OVERLAY.md + docs/JURISDICTION-OVERLAY-PLAN.md.
decisions.accuracy_mechanism: Every overlay and output carries the advisory-not-legal-determination boundary; genuine legal conflicts (e.g. historic frame vs HVHZ window) return human_review_required, never an auto-decision. No-fabrication: geometry overlays whose real boundary cannot be verified offline carry a geometry_ref to the authoritative endpoint plus a clearly-labeled illustrative bbox only (never a fabricated boundary); numeric values that are deployment-specific (SLR freeboard) are null-and-flagged. Attribute thresholds and statutory tests are cited facts (HVHZ FIPS, SB 4D 553.899, MRPA 3000+500, Buncombe 2500/3000/36%). The live tier is default-off and makes no network call unless both flags are on (proven by an exploding-getter selftest). Drift invariant 27 requires each record to be cited, kind-typed, and advisory-flagged.
decisions.explicit_non_action: Advisory only; never a legal or permitting determination. No heavy C GIS dependencies in the required path (stdlib + EPSG:4326; reprojection deferred via server-side outSR=4326). No scraping of Municode/American Legal, and no caching of copyrighted model-code text or NOAs (facts/citations only). Live GIS queries are default-off behind a separate flag; the whole core works fully offline. Rutherford County modeled honestly (no steep-slope ordinance / no countywide zoning) so the engine never falsely applies one.

decisions.decision: Hardened the jurisdictional-overlay architecture and loaded real, cited Orlando/Orange County data. Unified live-network consent (default-on, ask-first per session; headless-safe) across the FEMA flood lookup + US Census geocoding (geo_consent.py, geo_geocode.py); master jurisdictional_overlay switch default-on. An independent adversarial gate caught and fixed two safety-discard bugs in the conflict cascade (non-comparable stringency, and comparison across incommensurable units, now escalate to human review). Cached all 6 City of Orlando historic-district boundaries + the R-2B/T/HP zoning polygon as real GeoJSON with provenance via tools/geo_source_fetch.py; authored 11 cited overlay records; seeded 6 sources and stamped a currency baseline. Fixed a versioned-fact over-fire (rutherford scoped to its county) and excluded *.example.json schema demos from production resolution; invariant 27 now requires every versioned-fact to declare an applicability predicate.
decisions.rationale: P38: fix the bugs the 809 E Amelia test surfaced, prove the architecture correct before loading real data (tests + adversarial gate), then load accurate Orlando data (real values only within ~2 mi of Lake Eola; ToS-limited setback/wind values null-flagged, not fabricated). Advisory-not-legal-determination throughout; live network stays ask-first with no surprise call.

decisions.decision: Made every skill declare where/how it runs outside Claude, and packaged the jurisdiction overlay for non-Claude surfaces. shared/cross-modality-engine.md defines the model: three capability classes (A pure-reasoning, B offloadable to a public/hosted endpoint, C local-runtime), a per-surface matrix, a packaging map, and a degrade-never-fail fallback ladder. All 23 spokes carry a ## Cross-modality declaration; jurisdiction gets a GPT Action OpenAPI schema, Gemini function declarations, and docs/CROSS-MODALITY.md (access matrix). The setup wizard gained a /cross-modality screen that prints per-surface wiring steps and what runs there. Two new drift invariants: 28 (spokes declare cross-modality) and 29 (implementation/ schemas parse, which would have caught the GPT Action YAML typo).
decisions.rationale: P38-6/7: the ecosystem targets Claude Desktop/Code, claude.ai, Custom GPT, Gemini, and no-AI users; the ArcGIS/FEMA/Census endpoints do point-in-polygon server-side, so the universal path (call the public endpoint) reaches every surface except consumer Gemini Gems. Skills must know their class + mechanism so users can set them up per surface rather than hit a gap at use time.

decisions.decision: Audited and corrected the cross-modality declarations from P38-7 (which were assigned by heuristic). Re-derived each spoke's class from evidence (SKILL.md + workflow.json + composed atoms' tools + mcp_server.py). Two class fixes: analytics-insights B->C (runs roi-metric money math + docintel parsing) and partnership-mediakit A->B (depends on rate-benchmarks data); all other Class-C mechanism lines corrected to name the real tool module. Added an inherited one-line ## Cross-modality declaration to all 96 atoms. Hardened invariant 28 to require Class:/Runs on:/Mechanism:/Fallback: on spokes (no stub) + the line on atoms. Final: A=4, B=6, C=13. docs/CROSS-MODALITY-AUDIT.md records the per-skill verdicts + the packaging-candidate list (one remote-MCP deployment surfaces all Class-C skills; Class-B skills are knowledge-pack candidates).
decisions.rationale: P39: the P38-7 rollout was fast/heuristic; this makes each declaration evidence-true. The planned adversarial multi-agent audit hit an account session limit after 4 spokes and was completed as a deterministic self-audit incorporating those 4 agent findings; the full adversarial run is resumable when budget resets.

decisions.decision: Fixed all 10 flaws from the fictional CoolBreeze inbound-pitch test run. Finance: proposal_price accepts rate-floor-only (no_cost_basis gap) and one-of-two cost inputs never guesses (partial_cost_inputs); price_package sums per-item floors, excludes unpriceable items with a gap (never 0), flags the sum against a package benchmark. Rate card: pipeline/finance/rate-card.template.json committed (real card is gitignored rate-card.local.json; the P30-5 user-context template was merged in and removed), format-based floor resolution with rate_floor_source provenance, benchmark_tier_assumed/mismatch gaps. Benchmarks: five structure-only lever records (usage-rights/exclusivity uplifts, TikTok/Reel tier rates) with null values, source null, needs_research true, do-not-quote text; values arrive only via a later cited research pass. New atoms: product-fit (mandatory data_basis, exclusivity red flag caps the verdict) and pitch-extract (untrusted body, envelope-stamped citation, verbatim compensation, stage identified with inbound_pitch origin in stage_history; the 9-stage machine was deliberately NOT extended). Hub: pitch_triage classification routed to deal-pipeline chaining extract -> fit -> package price -> gap-record -> govern-artifact; contract drafting stays human-requested. Wizard: /brand-deals readiness screen with one-click local flag enable; contract-desk/finance-desk degraded messages name the exact flag and the wizard route; contract-draft output gains mandatory profile_gaps[]. Acceptance: 10/10 assertions (live CLI on a throwaway sandboxed rate card, removed after) plus scenario S7-coolbreeze-pitch with finance.price/finance.price_package ops added to scenario_check.py. Runbook docs/BRAND-DEALS.md.
decisions.rationale: The end-to-end test proved the safety spine held but the orchestration made the user do the chaining, pricing ergonomics forced dummy zeros, and benchmark levers had no records to cite. Structure-only benchmark records keep the no-fabrication line: named nulls beat invented ranges.

decisions.decision: Closed the two CoolBreeze re-run observations and opened the deliverable vocabulary. score.py evaluate() now rejects unknown dimension keys with an explicit error (the nine dimensions in protocols/quality-gates.md are exhaustive; a silently dropped score misleads the caller). finance.py suppresses the generic missing_input gap when the specific no_rate_card_entry gap fired for the same item, in both price_package and the --price CLI handler. The rate-card template documents format keys as open vocabulary and adds null rows for youtube_short, instagram_post_static, instagram_story_set, video_concept_ideas, script_only, and ugc_video_brand_channel; proposal-price, deal-pipeline, pitch-extract, and contract-draft document the mapping rule: map a deliverable to a format key only when unambiguous, otherwise unpriceable with a named gap, never forced into a video format; multi-deliverable agreements enumerate every deliverable. Regression-locked: 3 new finance selftest checks (99/99), a not_matches assertion check in scenario_check, an S4 unknown-dimension leg, and S7 extended to a mixed 3-type package.
decisions.rationale: Brands request arbitrary combinations of posts, video ideas, scripts, story sets, and UGC; the pricing path must degrade honestly per item instead of assuming the six template formats. Strict-unknown-keys keeps the quality gate deterministic.

decisions.decision: Built the creator document-template lane: block-structured, reusable templates for contracts, rate-card display docs, analytics overviews, and terms/conditions, with swappable clause blocks. Store: pipeline/templates/ gitignore-inverted (four all-null committed starters; real templates and attorney text only in gitignored .local files; drift invariant 31 enforces starter purity). Engine: shared/doc-template-engine.md defines the block model (kind, clause_family, fill_fields, applicability with advisory conditions plus code-enforced never_with/requires, variant groups for mutually exclusive alternatives) and the authorship boundary: the system never authors block body text; assembly is concatenation plus bracket substitution, proven by a byte-equality selftest. Tool: tools/doctemplates.py (stdlib, offline) validate/list/list-blocks/assemble/diff with fills from profile/deal/rate-card/export/manual sources, null-and-flag gaps, and writes gated by the new document_templates flag (contract types additionally behind the contract flags). Atoms: template-ingest (proposal-only, playbook-bootstrap discipline, exact-quote bodies via usage-rights-check, human saves by hand) and template-assemble (model selects whole blocks with reasons, code assembles; contract safety envelope preserved: banner, ready_to_sign false, profile_gaps). Wiring: template_manage hub classification to document-studio (template_proposal/from_template artifact types); contract-desk branches drafting to template-assemble when a vetted contract template exists, contract-draft stays the no-template path and reports availability. creator-profile.template.json gained legal_name/business_address/governing_law_state, closing the contract-draft profile_gaps mismatch. Regression: scenario S8 (swap the paid-usage variant in, exclude exclusivity, assert exact fixture sentences pass through and excluded text is absent) plus a 26-check tool selftest; wizard /brand-deals gained the flag switch and a saved-templates row.
decisions.rationale: Creators asked to upload contract examples and build reusable, creator-specific templates with individually swappable clauses. Separating template-assemble from contract-draft keeps the audited never-authors-legalese boundary intact: vetted text is creator-supplied and mechanically passed through, never generated.

decisions.decision: Fixed all 17 errors from the ChatGPT-surface audit and made the wizard walk non-technical users through any modality setup and transition. Single source of truth: shared/cross-modality/transitions.json (9 canonical surfaces incl. ChatGPT plain web, custom GPT, Projects, and the desktop app; 14 authored pair overrides; unverifiable OpenAI product claims tagged NEEDS VERIFICATION), mirrored by docs/TRANSITIONS.md and enforced by drift invariant 32 (matrix/doc/wizard-key consistency, packaging stamps, spoke-fallback ChatGPT tokens). Wizard: four-way welcome (Claude / ChatGPT / Gemini / more than one), a /chatgpt hub with per-flavor numbered steps, a /transitions screen rendering what travels, what stops working, and what to re-import for any pair (authored or honestly derived), a local-machine precondition banner on every writing screen, and page titles made em-dash-free. Honesty: every one-remote-MCP-endpoint-serves-ChatGPT claim reworded to CAN-serve-IF-deployed-behind-HTTPS-with-auth with the no-built-in-auth fact stated; deployer runbook at implementation/gpt/mcp-connector/README.md incl. ChatGPT web and desktop developer-mode registration. Reconciliation: connectors.json gained option_d2_chatgpt_connected (conditional Drive/MCP) resolving the freshness-matrix contradiction; the freshness screen states its choice only affects the local store. Profile import landed: implementation/gpt/profile-import prompt (per-context, per-field provenance) plus the proposal-only profile-import atom (named conflicts, injection containment, human saves) and provenance fields in the profile template. Privacy: docs/PASTE-SAFETY.md data classes, the DOCUMENT-TEMPLATES ChatGPT section (P42 code guarantees do not hold off-runtime), consent-asymmetry acknowledgment on the GPT Action, a flags-enforcement map, and packaging version stamps with a re-sync procedure. All 23 spoke Fallback lines now name their ChatGPT degradation path.
decisions.rationale: The audit showed OpenAI coverage was really custom-GPT-Action coverage, the ChatGPT desktop app was unknown to the repo, a load-bearing remote-MCP claim was documentation without an artifact, and no one owned transitions. The fixes ship runbooks, honest claims, and a data-driven transition guide rather than hosting or auth theater; unverifiable product facts are tagged, never asserted.

decisions.decision: Built the update story for an established user across Claude and ChatGPT, keyed to what each surface can actually do, verified against current (2026-07-12) vendor docs by an adversarial research pass. Self-update CHECK: tools/update_check.py polls the repo's own GitHub releases (reusing dependency_currency._http_get_json/fetch_latest/parse_version and version.read_versions) and reports current/behind/ahead against VERSION; it never pulls or writes code. Seeds a creator-os-release currency entry plus new Anthropic/OpenAI/MCP surface sources via seed-sources. NOTIFY: tools/update_notify.py gates the check behind an opt-in background_update_check capability (off by default; a headless/cron run with the flag off makes no network call) and emits ONE passive notice only when behind, always pointing at the explicit tools/update.py; FRESHNESS.md reconciles this with the never-nag rules (a user-enabled, non-repeating notice is a chosen setting, not a nag). Surfaces: wizard /updates screen (version, one-click enable, per-surface guidance) and MCP get_server_info + update_check tools plus a quiet startup line. DATA SAFETY (consent-first): tools/local_audit.py is the read-only schema audit (one quiet line, details on demand, writes nothing); CHANGELOG.migrations.json carries a human-authored why_it_matters + concrete_impact per data-shape schema_version (no-fabrication) and drift invariant 33 requires an entry for every pipeline template's current schema_version; tools/migrate_local.py provides the safe read-time compat_view default (missing fields read as null, existing values untouched) and an opt-in, per-file/per-field consented, reversible write migration (backup + atomic write + byte-for-byte rollback to gitignored .local logs). DISTRIBUTION: the git-backed .claude-plugin marketplace gives Claude Code/Cowork background updates (documented: third-party marketplaces default auto-update off; Cowork org Sync-automatically fires only on a version-bump PR merge; the Cowork sandbox is ephemeral so .local data must be externalized). Hosted remote-MCP is the only true-background browser path: the mcp-connector runbook and CROSS-MODALITY state the rule that the tool set must stay small and stable (content flows through responses) because neither claude.ai nor ChatGPT reliably picks up a changed tool contract mid-session, with serverInfo.version as the poll signal and OAuth 2.1 (RFC 9728/8707/PKCE) deployer requirements. ChatGPT: TRANSITIONS documents frozen paste (re-upload; no management API) vs a stable Action/dev-mode connector (server-side live) vs agent mode (prep-only, human pastes). docs/UPDATING.md is the consolidated per-surface runbook.
decisions.rationale: The user asked to keep Claude and ChatGPT users updated in the background to the fullest extent each modality allows, for non-technical users, while keeping private data local and never touching GitHub on their behalf. The research showed the honest ceilings differ sharply by surface (true-background only via plugin auto-update or a hosted connector; pasted knowledge is always a frozen re-upload; consumer GPT/Project knowledge has no write API), so the plan builds a read-only consent-gated check, a reversible consent-first data-migration path, and per-surface distribution guidance, tagging every unverifiable vendor claim NEEDS VERIFICATION rather than asserting it.

decisions.decision: Built the content-import lane: import, complete, and analyze the creator's OWN past videos + metadata across YouTube/Instagram/TikTok/Pinterest, entirely on the creator's machine, proposal-only. Grounded in verified 2026-07-12 API/CSV/export field shapes (Appendix A of the approved plan). Two corrected premises are load-bearing: YouTube snippet.tags are PUBLIC (not owner-only), and a solo creator CANNOT pull revenue via the YouTube Analytics API (content-owner reports only), so revenue enters ONLY via the YouTube Studio CSV. STORE: tools/video_library.py is a local gitignored SQLite+FTS5 store (upsert by video_key; each stat wrapped in a freshness envelope; retention null off YouTube; no committed summary export) plus a read-only analysis layer (top tags, YouTube retention peaks/cliffs, format performance, transcript themes; every figure cites video_keys; unavailable data null-flagged). PARSERS: tools/import_parse.py reads the four platforms' export bundles (revenue only from the Studio CSV). LIVE TIER: tools/importers/* is a flag-gated OAuth REST client (content_import_live master + per-platform read flag + own creds; injected-getter selftests; the YouTube importer builds no monetary URL and skips ASR captions). COMPLETION: tools/transcribe.py is an OS/backend-aware on-device STT runner (whisper.cpp on Apple Silicon via select_backend, faster-whisper elsewhere; RAM-tiered model floor; run_local_stt gap with the per-OS install when no backend, never a fabricated transcript); tools/library_complete.py matches downloaded media to records, drives per-video completion, and joins the YouTube retention curve to the transcript so each most-watched moment carries the words spoken there (elapsed_ratio x duration_s). SKILLS: video-import, transcript-import, library-complete, library-analyze atoms + the content-library spoke (Class C with the invariant-32 Cross-modality Fallback and per-surface re-route). WIRING: hub routes import_past_videos/analyze_back_catalog/most_watched_parts; MCP video_library_query + video_library_import_status; the wizard /import guided flow with per-OS STT install + macOS Gatekeeper/Python notes. GOVERNANCE: drift invariant 34 (video-library starters are pure null shape), scenario S9 (import Studio CSV + IG DYI -> analyze; most-watched from retention, revenue CSV-only, IG retention null-flagged), docs/CONTENT-IMPORT.md + SETUP_MAC.md STT section + DEPLOYMENT.md matrix rows. Boundaries: retention YouTube-only, revenue Studio-CSV-only, transcripts on-device or uploaded caption (ASR not downloaded, no scraping), all data gitignored, importer proposes and the human saves.
decisions.why: An established creator's back catalog was an unused first-party asset: Creator OS scanned competitors but had no store, importer, or analysis for the creator's own videos. The build makes it end-to-end for a non-technical user while never fabricating a stat, retention, revenue, or transcript, and never letting the creator's private performance data leave their machine.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Live-API-only import (rejected: revenue is not available via API and OAuth verification is a distribution burden; the export bundle is the reliable floor). A cloud transcription service (rejected: the creator's audio and transcripts must stay local, zero-token). Estimating retention off YouTube (rejected: no-fabrication; null-and-flag instead).

decisions.decision: Hardened the P45 content-import lane and added a non-technical onboarding layer after a read-only stress test (simulated MacBook Pro on Claude web/Cowork/Desktop) surfaced 9 defects concentrated in the live importers and the export-ZIP parser. Fixes, each grounded in cited platform behavior: (1) TikTok create_time coerced via _epoch_to_iso (int64 epoch-seconds per the Video Object ref) so a malformed field never aborts the batch; (2+3) YouTube/Instagram/TikTok pagination bounded by a max_pages backstop returning a truncated sentinel instead of a silent partial library, with Instagram terminating on the documented paging.next absence (not the after cursor, which can persist on the final page) and the URL pinned to v25.0; (4) import_parse opens every zip via _safe_zip catching OSError AND zipfile.BadZipFile (verified NOT an OSError subclass) so a corrupt export degrades to []; (5+6) video_library CLI returns a clean JSON error + nonzero exit on malformed upsert/FTS input; (7) derive_most_watched returns [] on a flat retention curve instead of labeling the whole video a peak; (8) library_complete joins whisper-JSON/SRT transcripts to retention and flags an untimed transcript with a no_timing gap; (9) complete --write reports honest per-field write counts. Non-technical layer: tools/transcribe.py doctor gives a green/amber/red readiness verdict + the exact next command per OS, and --fetch-model streams a whisper.cpp GGML model (stdlib urllib, env proxy + CA bundle) verified against a committed sha256 allowlist (canonical-sources/whisper-models.json, captured from Hugging Face git-LFS object ids since whisper.cpp publishes no manifest); the wizard adds a /doctor screen with one-click verified model downloads + corrupt-export recovery copy; docs/SETUP_MAC.md gains doctor + Windows (SmartScreen, Add-to-PATH, faster-whisper, prebuilt whisper.cpp) sections. Drift invariant 35 (check_importer_robustness) AST-checks that the defect class cannot regress: no unbounded while-True pagination, every zipfile.ZipFile inside a try, create_time fromtimestamp inside a try, and a truncation signal preserved.
decisions.why: P45 shipped the capability but the live importers and ZIP parser were the least defensive surfaces, and installing a local STT backend was the biggest real blocker for a non-technical creator. This makes the widest range of non-technical users (macOS first, then Windows) succeed while keeping every guarantee: local-only, never fabricated, importer proposes and the human saves.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Catching only OSError on zip open (rejected: BadZipFile is not an OSError subclass, verified). A pure substring drift check for int()/while-True (rejected: too many false positives; used a scoped AST check instead). Silent page caps like TikTok's original (rejected: violates no-silent-cap; a truncation sentinel is surfaced). Bundling model weights (rejected: large + licensing; ship only the cited sha256 allowlist and download+verify on demand).

decisions.decision: Added a diagnose-only currency/versioning/push-integrity layer: tools/preflight_push.py (read-only push-blocker predictor) and tools/release.py + release.yml (ready, not auto-firing release wiring). Grew the drift guard to 45 invariants: 36 catalog-integrity keystone (single source of truth for the invariant catalog; fixed the double-'Invariant 22' + stale header), 37-38 error-level (legal-source category, marketplace/plugin version equality), 39-45 advisory (non-blocking). Corrected the three/five registry-writer doc drift across registry_io.py, CLAUDE.md, and CURRENCY.md, and the stale currency-report CI reference. Fixed the one real recurring CI failure (competitor-intel continues on an empty snapshot dir). Staged a cited July-2026 volatile-correction backlog + moving-date calendar + EU AI Act seed, none applied.
decisions.rationale: Automated guarantees stopped at coverage and shape, not currency; version/identity numbers advanced by hand with nothing linking them; the only recurring red-X was structural (empty competitor-snapshot dir). Diagnose-only per the approved plan: detection + a cited, prioritized correction backlog with exact unexecuted apply commands, one approved mutation (the baseline release) left as a hand-off because this environment has no gh and no release API.
decisions.verification: Drift clean at 45 invariants (1 intended advisory: the NY law effective 2026-06-09). preflight_push/release/dependency_currency/update_check selftests green; scenarios 9/9; every new invariant negative-tested; the mcp>=1.28,<2 correction verified live against PyPI (stable 1.28.1, breaking 2.0.0a1+ present).

decisions.decision: Added a before-a-release fallback + channel model to the self-update lane. When no GitHub release is published, tools/update_check.py compares the installed commit against a tracked branch (one read-only GitHub compare call) and reports behind_unreleased/current/ahead. A named channel (stable->main, nightly->experimental branch) resolves to a branch via resolve_channel(); tools/update.py pulls the same resolved branch; the wizard /updates screen gained a channel selector writing creator-os-config.local.json.
decisions.rationale: The version 0.1.0 is stamped internally but no release/tag artifact exists yet, so the release-only poll returned no_release and the self-update path was inert. The user asked for a backup based on the most recent branch updates, framed as stable vs nightly channels. Read-only, never-nag, opt-in; reverts to release comparison automatically once a release is cut.
decisions.verification: update_check --selftest 40/40 (every edge case: identical/behind/ahead/diverged/compare-404/no-git/offline/truncated/release-exists, plus classify_compare + resolve_channel units); update_notify --selftest 15/15; wizard renders the selector and _update_config_section merges into .local.json; drift clean at 45 invariants; the GitHub compare-direction inversion (base=local, head=branch: 'ahead' => local behind => behind_unreleased) verified.

decisions.decision: Remediated a full-repo audit across nine workstreams. WS9: a bot-blocked source is now classified 'blocked' (inconclusive), never demoted to stale/changed/gone/orphan; source_currency wires fetch_diag.classify_block, records durable last_block_detected, and excludes blocked sources from staleness/SLA; traversal orphan-pruning and dependency_currency/update_check distinguish a rate-limit from an absence; an all-surface human/browser verification handoff plus an opt-in --resilient retry were added. WS1 decodes YouTube categoryId to a label. WS5/6 add a way-home source URL to every knowledge stamp and a single combined knowledge pack. WS8 adds a completeness-contract doc and softens overclaiming GPT Action strings. WS4 adds a persona-audit protocol + read-only harness + first run + low-risk wizard fixes. Three new drift invariants took the count 45->48: 46 URL provenance (ERROR), 47 knowledge-pack projection staleness (advisory), 48 doc-count truth (ERROR). Stale doc counts corrected (22 spokes, 5 agent roles, 48 invariants).
decisions.rationale: Match the Teacher OS discipline: known things are declared and the build fails on undeclared ones; outputs stay honest about coverage; a source that gets bot-blocked is not silently discarded; and doc counts cannot drift stale unnoticed.
decisions.verification: sync_check clean at 48 invariants; scenario_check 9/9; selftests source_currency 28/28, video_library 30/30, youtube_import 19/19, freshness_bundle 14/14, projection 6/6, persona 5/5, dependency 21/21, update_check 40/40; negative tests confirm invariants 46/47/48 fire and clear.

decisions.decision: Built out five structural persona-audit stumbles into the wizard onboarding. (5) No-terminal launch: double-click Start Creator OS Setup.command (executable bit + Gatekeeper note) and .bat (SmartScreen note) launchers, plus a launch_setup MCP tool. (6) First-screen IA collapsed to one primary AI question with a /claude browser-vs-desktop chooser and a /bring 'bring what you already have' hub. (7) Native-first Google: _screen_google leads with the built-in connector and demotes Google Cloud Console to an advanced expander; a /storage-folder step registers a filesystem MCP scoped to one chosen folder. (10) Guided import: _screen_import reworked into ask-Claude plus a Scan/preview/Approve form (/api/run-import) with raw commands in an expander. (11) Default install: tools/setup.py --install-deps and a wizard 'Set up my computer' screen install every free pip set + uv + Playwright's browser with honest per-package reporting; _screen_node_missing gains inline recovery + a re-check button. Blockers: configure-stats-tool reconciled to the canonical MCP registry; numpy/python-dateutil/sqlite-vec/PyYAML declared and seeded for invariant 23; new docs/DEPENDENCIES.md.
decisions.rationale: A non-technical creator (Alex) could not start setup without a terminal, faced nine first-screen choices, hit the Google Cloud Console wall, and met raw shell commands to import. These were the highest-friction abandon points; each is now guided, batteries-included, and honest (propose-then-confirm preserved; nothing keyed/paid installed by default; every degrade null-and-flag).
decisions.verification: sync_check clean at 48 invariants; scenario_check 9/9; persona_audit 5/5 across 23 screens (0 orphans, 0 token leaks); import_parse 20/20; video_library 30/30; scan->approve import flow exercised end to end against synthetic IG+TikTok exports into a sandboxed store; installer result shape unit-tested (9 items, valid JSON).

decisions.decision: Built real publishing OAuth + gated live upload for all four platforms (persona-audit stumbles 8, 9) plus a native folder picker (10). Shared tools/oauth_flow.py runs a per-platform loopback OAuth flow capturing the researched divergences: PKCE base64url for Google, HEX for TikTok, none (confidential) for Pinterest/Instagram; body vs HTTP Basic client auth; TikTok refresh-token rotation persisted. Generalized wizard callback /oauth/<platform>/callback with single-use state CSRF, /api/oauth-start, and a manual paste-the-code fallback (Instagram loopback acceptance is unverified). Fixed the credential clobber: _merge_api_credentials deep-merges and publishing tokens live under creds[plat].publish so they never overwrite importer read tokens; Instagram ig_user_id canonicalized at the root. Live clients (tools/publishing/, all gated behind live_publishing_enabled default-off + human confirmation, all built on an injected transport with zero-network selftests): YouTube resumable upload (default-private, upload-only, provably no monetary/analytics endpoint); Pinterest base64 image Pin (no public URL); TikTok creator_info->init->chunked FILE_UPLOAD->status (SELF_ONLY default, refuses unaudited public, is_aigc); Instagram container->poll->media_publish surfacing the public-URL wall and professional-account requirement honestly instead of faking a local upload. Decision on YouTube: no website-free Production path exists, so the honest flow is Testing mode + ~7-day re-auth, stated on the screen. Native folder picker tools/pick_folder.py (tkinter-in-subprocess + osascript/PowerShell-STA/zenity/kdialog fallbacks + headless degrade) wired as a Browse button on the import and storage screens; the text field stays the floor. Deprecated OOB flow deliberately never implemented. Compliance has_credentials tightened to the publish namespace. Added docs/PUBLISHING.md, an integrations-engine section, and a live-account verification checklist. Drift clean; scenarios 9/9; persona audit 0 orphans, no token leaks.

Closed the maintainer/doc accuracy blind spot in the drift guard (seo-tools only). Added forward guards so prose cannot silently diverge from code: extend the path-resolution check to docs/*.md and README.md; a symbol-reference invariant (<!-- verify: path::symbol --> resolved against the AST, allowlist for dynamic/optional symbols); a tools-layer maintainer-coverage invariant (TOOLS_MAINTAINER_DIRS); and tools/doc_freshness.py content-hash staleness stamping (advisory). Created tools/publishing/MAINTAINER_README.md. Ran a full multi-agent, cite-or-drop content-accuracy sweep and, after human review, applied only the approved corrections; lowered the skill-template regression bar to three and backfilled Regression sections on five atoms; retrofitted 61 verify: markers across 43 docs. Added process conventions: advisory .github/CODEOWNERS (@flywifi), docs/adr/ (MADR; 5 foundational + 31 reformatted from this ledger), root CHANGELOG.md (Keep a Changelog + SemVer, reconstructed from STATE + ledger), docs/DOC-MAINTENANCE.md, and a CLAUDE.md same-PR docs rule.
decisions.rationale: The guard verified path resolution and integer counts but nothing checked that maintainer/SKILL/docs prose agreed with code behavior, so stale scopes, 'stub' claims, and endpoint drift shipped green. P51 both created an instance of that drift (the publishing __init__ docstring) and exposed the gap; the credentialed tools/ layer had zero maintainer or guard coverage.
evidence: tools/sync_check.py (drift clean; new symbol-ref, tools-maintainer, path-to-docs checks)
tools/count_truth.py (22 spokes / 105 atoms / 129 skills / 51 invariants / 9 scenarios)
regression probe: a bad verify marker and a reintroduced '14 spokes' count each fail sync_check (exit 1); restore -> exit 0
decisions.explicit_non_action: seo-tools only (educator-tools-k12-public excluded entirely, no reads/edits); no product-code behavior changes (guards are new checks only); dated historical audits annotated, not rewritten; sweep prose edits applied only after review; staleness stamping labeled emerging-practice, not a standard; CODEOWNERS advisory (no branch-protection change).

decisions.decision: Fixed the macOS issues found by the P53 stress test (code + copy; behavioral confirmation stays the hands-on checklist). Core mechanism: a private per-repo .venv (tools/env_paths.py app_python/which; setup.py ensure_venv installs deps there) resolves the PEP 668 installer break on Homebrew Python, the double-click GUI-PATH gap (brew prefixes prepended), and the Claude Desktop absolute-interpreter requirement together. Also: wizard _os()/_arch() sim seam, port-8765 friendly-exit (no traceback), _mcp_command absolute npx/uvx resolution + Quit-completely/log copy, launcher .command real-python probe + brew PATH + Tahoe Open-Anyway copy, arch-aware STT copy, TCC folder-denial message, DaVinci multi-path, Safari OAuth caveat. New --selftest guards: loopback-127.0.0.1 (G1), whisper 3-name probe (G2), macOS render, port collision.
decisions.rationale: P53 confirmed two HIGH code defects (PEP 668 silently installs nothing on Homebrew Python; brew tools invisible under a double-click launch) plus F3-F16 code/copy gaps. The user chose the private-.venv approach, which is best-practice isolation and consolidates F1/F2/F3 under one mechanism with graceful fallback (no .venv == prior behavior).
evidence: tools/wizard.py --selftest (macOS render seam + port-collision + loopback G1 + whisper G2)
decisions.explicit_non_action: Cannot change macOS itself: real Gatekeeper/TCC/Homebrew-PEP668/Safari/Rosetta behavior stays the P53 hands-on checklist, verified here only at the mechanism level. .venv degrades to prior behavior when absent. seo-tools only; educator repo untouched. No product-logic change beyond launch/install/detect plumbing + copy.

decisions.decision: Seeded the 23 macOS/AI-surface research sources from the P53/P54 work into canonical-sources/source-registry.json (new os-platform category + ai-surface-spec; registry 218 to 241; every URL fetch-verified pre-seed; freshness bundle restamped) and built the declare-generate-enforce citation trigger: a doc declares its external authorities in a fenced sources block or an inline source: marker; tools/source_sync.py (read-only) reconciles declarations against the registry and generates the seed file for anything unregistered; new fail-closed drift invariant 52 (check_doc_source_registry) fails the build when a declared id is unregistered, its URL disagrees with the registry, or a block is unparseable, with illustrative-id exemptions in tools/doc-source-allowlist.json. Declared-sources blocks added to docs/MACOS-MAINTENANCE.md and docs/SETUP_MAC.md; two macOS platform dates added to moving-dates.json.
decisions.rationale: The P53/P54 macOS work cited ~22 authoritative sources in prose, but none were registered, so the currency system never freshness-checked them, and invariant 46 covers code URLs only - a doc citation triggered nothing. The user chose the hybrid declare+generate+enforce design, fail-closed like invariant 23 (the requirements-to-registry precedent). The generator stays read-only and the human runs seed-sources, so the sanctioned five-writer registry set is unchanged (invariant 42). Pre-seed verification: a 22-agent URL fetch pass (20 confirmed exact, 2 content mismatches fixed at entry level: TN3179 states the loopback exemption only by implication, so the explicit DTS FAQ was added as a 23rd source; the Claude Desktop local-MCP support article was rewritten around .mcpb extensions, so its entry was re-pointed) plus a line-cited code accuracy pass (allowlist location, check_freshness_bundle restamp, count-truth ripple to 52, the inv-42 textual writer-scan trap, the ai-surface-spec 30/30 interval override).

decisions.decision: Ran an independent adversarial audit of the trailing-24h change set (P50 to P55) with sandboxed zero-network harnesses, confirmed 12 findings (all gated behind live_publishing_enabled=OFF, so no user was exposed), and remediated all of them in P57 with each finding's harness flipped from CONFIRMED to KILLED and the green baseline preserved. Publish path: dashboard now passes the full creds map to dispatch (F1); dispatch structurally enforces the live flag + explicit human confirm instead of trusting the caller (F2/F8); a persist callable is threaded so a TikTok refresh-token rotation is saved (F3); the scheduler dispatches only a direct_api-tier platform (F7); add-to-queue strips caller-supplied control fields (F8). OAuth: Pinterest/Instagram redirect host localhost to 127.0.0.1 (F9). Wizard: home-tree path confinement on the import scan and the filesystem-MCP folder (F4/F6); a cross-site Origin/Referer guard on every mutating POST (F5); nightly_branch git-ref validation + HTML escaping (F10/F11). Added the missing publishing_compliance --selftest (F12).
decisions.rationale: P51 shipped the repo's first credentialed, network-touching code and P50 a large browser-driven wizard; the author's own selftests passed on the way in but had not been independently attacked. The audit found the live publish path had never worked end to end (F1) and its network gate was a caller convention rather than a structural invariant (F2), plus a browser-CSRF/arbitrary-read surface on the loopback wizard (F4/F5/F6). Fixes make the safety properties structural and confine the wizard, without enabling live publishing (still OFF by default). The LOW hardening cluster is drafted-not-applied; a fresh map of three surfaces not reached under a session limit (launch/install, the P52 guards, a P55 regression pass) is deferred.

decisions.decision: Remediated the LOW hardening cluster the P56 audit deferred and closed the audit's own coverage gap. OAuth (A1): explicit urllib.error import; refresh() treats a transient 400 as a retryable OAuthError while 401/terminal codes force reconnect; a dead Instagram long-lived token raises ReauthRequired. Clients (A2): reject a 0-byte file before any network init; pin the YouTube resumable-PUT to a googleapis host; Instagram requires a real ig_user_id (dropped the account_id/Page-id fallback). Honesty (A3): ftc_disclosure_verified documented as a presence check, not content validation. Wizard (A4): bounded/malformed-safe request body; back up a corrupt Claude config before overwrite; allowlist the fetch-model tier; single-use token on the import batch to stop cross-tab approval. Deferred: ThreadingMixIn (A4d part 2) would add concurrency to the unlocked config-file RMW paths P57 hardened. Track B mapped+verified the three session-limited surfaces (launch/install, the P52 guards as oracles, P55) and found no new issues.
decisions.rationale: P57 fixed everything exploitable and left a labeled backlog + a coverage hole. The LOW items are real robustness/honesty gaps that should not ship in credentialed network code even when not exploitable today; Track B proves the un-mapped surfaces are clean rather than assuming it. Publishing stays OFF by default; no feature change. The one deferral is a conscious risk trade recorded in the ADR.

decisions.decision: Audited four surfaces (versioning, maintainer files, README/docs, error logs) for currency and accuracy, aggregating cited research from four read-only agents and re-verifying every load-bearing lead directly before acting. Fixed: the versions.json updated stamp (16 days stale, unvalidated by any guard); publishing maintainer invariants 2 and 3 (still described the pre-P57 caller-only gate and the pre-P58 account_id fallback); seven stale finance.py selftest pass-counts across six finance atom docs (44/59/71 vs the actual 99); docs/WIZARD.md (loopback bind + the P57/P58 request guards); docs/ARCHITECTURE.md (the atoms table presented 27 of 105 installed atoms as the full inventory); and the dashboard scheduler, which recorded any ok:false client refusal outside gated/unconfirmed/auth_required as published with a null post id (now keyed on ok via _apply_dispatch_result, pinned by a new dashboard --selftest). Re-blessed the doc-freshness manifest (invariant 51 green) and bound the finance docs to finance.py so the stale-count class is machine-caught. Recorded as stale-by-decision, unchanged: the CHANGELOG [0.1.0] heading vs the not-yet-published release (and autoUpdate), the unstamped creator-os-release registry entry, and the staged volatile corrections (invariant 43 keeps firing by accepted choice).
decisions.rationale: The automated guards check the version triple and global counts but are blind to dated stamps, selftest pass-counts quoted in prose, release-heading truth, and result-mapping honesty; two advisory signals were firing uncleared. Per review decisions, the release claim and the staged currency corrections stay as-is with documented hand-off commands rather than being changed in-environment.

decisions.decision: Built the omnichannel Drive hub (ADR 0043): one shared Drive folder (Inbox, Store, Jobs, Knowledge, Profile, Outbox; spec docs/DRIVE-HUB.md) under an append-only/create-only write rule, with the async compute hand-off as ONE queue and runner (tools/handoff/: job-type allowlist, UUID idempotency, hub-confined input paths, per-type timeouts, honest failures) fed by three transports: the Drive-for-desktop watcher (default), opt-in Drive API polling (drive.file, create-only), and opt-in remote MCP submit_compute_job/job_status (58 tools). The drop folder ships as the inbox-routing atom (atoms 105 to 106) over the inbox_rules.json dispatch table and tools/handoff/inbox.py (scan read-only and never guesses content categories; approve is the sole writer with sha256 idempotency), the wizard /inbox flow, and scenario S10 (scenarios 9 to 10, routing pinned absent like profile-import). Projects get the dual projection (tools/project_docs.py): pack copies into Knowledge/ plus an opt-in Google Docs lane updating stable doc ids so a private Project live-syncs; engines-to-pack staleness stays invariant 47, pack-to-Drive is the tool's own check because CI cannot see Drive. All three hub capabilities default OFF; no job path can post, publish, or read credentials. Real-Mac behaviors are the ADR 0043 hands-on checklist, verified at mechanism level only.
alternatives_rejected: Drive files.watch push notifications (needs a public webhook a local tool must not require)
a resident watcher daemon (the cron/launchd --once convention is the repo standard)
registering the Drive copies in PROJECTIONS/invariant 47 (CI cannot observe Drive)
a hub routing row for inbox-routing (standalone atoms trigger on their own descriptions)

decisions.decision: Closed every P60 open item addressable without a Mac (ADR 0044). Six locked decisions: two-step work orders with amendments carried as consent_note data (A-CONFIRM2); the offline injection pattern tier screening ALL ingested documents with matched phrasing shown for human review (SEC-ALL); a real offline keyword tool over the full library, volumes always null (KW-FULL); queue-and-wait while the compute switch is off with the switch state visible at multiple points (GATE-QUEUE); proposal-only jobs with direct saves behind an acknowledged local capability the runner re-reads (WRITE-OPTIN); and a sealed, logged Inbox/Quarantine area behind four resurrection locks (Q-SEAL). Also fixed the broken library_complete builder, the transcribe output location, and the project_docs token refresh; delivered report jobs to the Outbox on both transports; gave mcp_server its first selftest (two tiers, live 58 == static). Capabilities 61 to 62.
alternatives_rejected: auto-queueing follow-up work on approval (rejected by user: the human and machine must agree on the exact work list first)
parsing or acting on the amendment text (rejected: it would become an instruction channel; it travels as data only)
flag-in-place quarantine (rejected: a flagged trap left in the working folder can be re-scanned; the sealed area cannot)

decisions.decision: Made the offline injection pattern tier a genuine FIRST pass whose verdict feeds the authoritative in-session semantic guard (ADR 0045). The offline result is an advisory prior rendered as category+score (never raw content), wrapped with the content in an <untrusted_content> envelope (data, not instructions); the session writes the authoritative injection_scan_result and a reconciliation {agreed, session_action: confirmed|escalated|downgraded, effective, pass_coverage, note}. approve persists the {offline_pattern_scan, injection_scan_result, reconciliation} triple and refuses to route anything the offline tier sealed (SEAL-TERMINAL) or the session escalated. Coverage varies by modality (both/offline_only/session_only); ChatGPT/Gemini packaging instructs the discipline, not enforces it. Decisions ENG-ALL, RECONCILE-FULL, SEAL-TERMINAL.
alternatives_rejected: keeping the two tiers independent (status quo) -- rejected: the cheap layer never informed the authoritative one
AI auto-re-examination of sealed files -- rejected (SEAL-TERMINAL): releasing a false positive is a deliberate human move
treating a single pass as authoritative in isolation -- rejected per OWASP LLM01: no single pass is sufficient (regex misses rewordings; a model judging injected content is itself injectable)

decisions.decision: Fixed the four defects the 2026-07-18 diagnose-only sweep confirmed, each locked by a proof mechanism that fails on the pre-fix code (ADR 0046). (1) Connector resolver: google_drive_hub gains default_flag not_installed (the only 1 of 39 without it; every requires_capability peer uses that default) and resolve()/cmd_list() read the key via .get() so a malformed entry degrades to off; NEW invariant 53 executes resolve() over the committed registry, fail-closed. (2) transcript_normalize: the runner's three-flag argv silently collapsed to gap metrics (the transcripts CLI modes are mutually exclusive); a new ADDITIVE --normalize mode emits the one combined object (segments + silences + chapters, per-mode min-gap defaults preserved) and the runner selftest now RUNS the built argv on the committed workshop-footage fixture asserting all three keys; single-mode arms byte-unchanged for the footage-analysis atom (decision TRANS-FLAG; rejected: combined-on-multi-flag, runner-merges-3-calls). (3) finance/obligations error posture: both payload loaders raise a tagged PayloadError that main() converts to the {error,next_step} envelope with exit 1 across all ten payload entry points, and build_invoice gap-flags a non-dict terms as malformed_terms; NEW invariant 54 AST-asserts both guards stay (decision GUARD-BOTH). Invariants 52 to 54; counts otherwise unchanged. Selftests: transcripts 7/7 (new), runner 27/27, finance 102/102, obligations 16/16. Negative tests confirmed both new invariants fire on the pre-fix tree.
alternatives_rejected: combined-output-on-multi-flag for transcripts.py (implicit contract keyed to flag count)
runner merges three subprocess calls (3x process cost + merge logic outside the tool)
selftests without the two drift invariants (no build-time lock on the defect classes)
